pi_items.source: Valid desktop path on Linux system. Task description specifies this path for output.
Task description (line 5 of task.json) explicitly states 'A client named Margaret Chen'. The name appears plausible as a fictional client name.

pi_items.verified_value: Approximately 460-500 miles, 9-11 hours driving time. Sources cite 460-500 miles as standard range, with 9-11 hours for continuous driving without stops.
pi_items.source: Multiple travel guides and official route planning sources confirm Pacific Coast Highway from San Francisco to Los Angeles is approximately 460-500 miles and takes 9-11 hours of continuous driving.

pi_items.metadata_value: 20 waypoints including Santa Cruz, Monterey, Carmel, Big Sur, San Simeon, Hearst Castle, Cambria, Morro Bay, San Luis Obispo, Pismo Beach, Santa Maria, Solvang, Santa Barbara, Ventura, Malibu, Pacific Palisades, Half Moon Bay, Paso Robles, Bixby Creek Bridge, McWay Falls
pi_items.verified_value: 14 waypoints are authentic PCH locations/landmarks. 3 waypoints (Santa Maria, Solvang, Paso Robles) are INLAND and NOT on Highway 1/PCH: Santa Maria is 10 miles inland served by US-101; Solvang is inland accessed via State Route 246 from US-101; Paso Robles is inland on US-101, with Highway 46 connecting to Highway 1.
pi_items.source: Web search of Pacific Coast Highway routes and individual location verification. Sources: (1) Santa Maria is 10 miles inland per loyaltytraveler.boardingarea.com; (2) Solvang is inland via State Route 246 per Wikipedia and Route planning guides; (3) Paso Robles on US-101 inland per AARoads.com and California State Route information.

pi_items.metadata_value: All 20 waypoints listed are on or near Pacific Coast Highway
pi_items.verified_value: Authentic Pacific Coast Highway stops verified: Santa Cruz, Monterey, Carmel, Big Sur, San Simeon, Cambria, Morro Bay, San Luis Obispo, Pismo Beach, Santa Barbara, Ventura, Malibu, Pacific Palisades, Half Moon Bay, Hearst Castle (near San Simeon), Bixby Creek Bridge (Big Sur), McWay Falls (Big Sur). Total: 17 authentic PCH locations.
pi_items.source: Comprehensive web search of Pacific Coast Highway stops and individual verification of each location's geography relative to Highway 1.

pi_items.verified_value: Both domains are valid Google Maps access points
pi_items.source: Web search confirmation of Google Maps as major mapping service with multiple domain entry points.

pi_items.verified_value: All four are valid, real mapping services: Bing Maps (Microsoft), MapQuest, Waze (Google-owned), and OpenStreetMap (open-source community project)
pi_items.source: Web search verification of mapping services industry.

pi_items.verified_value: Both are real weather services: weather.gov is US National Weather Service (government), weather.com is The Weather Channel
pi_items.source: Web search confirmation of weather services.

pi_items.verified_value: All four are valid weather services: AccuWeather (commercial), Weather Underground, OpenWeatherMap (API service with 2M+ customers), BBC Weather (UK broadcaster)
pi_items.source: Web search verification of weather services industry.

privileged_info_summary: The Pacific Coast Highway route from San Francisco to Los Angeles spans approximately 460-500 miles and takes 9-11 hours of continuous driving. The task requires researching this scenic coastal route and compiling an itinerary for client Margaret Chen. The metadata lists 20 waypoints, but 3 of them (Santa Maria, Solvang, and Paso Robles) are INLAND locations served by US-101, not on Highway 1/Pacific Coast Highway proper. The remaining 17 waypoints are authentic Pacific Coast Highway stops or landmarks. All mapping services (Google Maps, Bing Maps, MapQuest, Waze, OpenStreetMap) and weather services (Weather.gov, Weather.com, AccuWeather, Weather Underground, OpenWeatherMap, BBC Weather) listed in metadata are real, existing services.
